violations.text: The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2025

Raw animal foods (fish) were observed stored above ready-to-eat foods (fries) in the prep unit. Raw animal foods must be separated from raw ready-to-eat food such as vegetables during storage. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener, slicer, and pans) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2025

ROP tuna was observed thawing in refrigerator with package still intact.ROP seafood must be removed from package prior to thawing. PIC informed and voluntarily disposed of food product. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2025

The presence of (rodents ) is not controlled as evidenced by (droppings in basement ). The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2025
(Various food product ) were observed stored on the floor in the (walk in cooler and basement). Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2025

Beverage tubing was observed in contact with ice used for drinks at the bar. Beverage tubing and cold plate beverage cooling devices must not be in contact with stored ice.PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2025

The following non-food contact surfaces have an accumulation of debris: 1.grill shelves 2.all prep units 3.walk in cooler food storage racks 4.stoves hoods 5.walk in cooler fans 6.prep table shelves 7.kitchen equipment(blenders, waffle maker, etc) Non food contact surfaces must be kept clean as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2025

The floors and walls throughout the facility have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris(including floors in basement). The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2025

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the ( kitchen and basement) area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2025

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

An employee was observed touching ready to eat food (cucumbers) with bare hands. Food employees must not touch exposed, ready-to-eat food with their bare hands and shall use suitable utensils such as deli tissue, spatulas, single use gloves, or dispensing equipment. Product disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

Shellstock (mussels) located in (walk in cooler) did not have the proper identification tags. Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the shellstock are received until the container is empty.PIC informed and placed tags in container with shellstock. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener and ice) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Unit taken out of service. Ice voluntarily disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was (25) parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Unit taken out of service and technician was called during the inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024
Various food product observed with date marking exceeding the 7 days after date of preparation.PIC voluntarily disposed of food product. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024

Working containers of (dry ingredients) are not labeled with the common name of the food. Working containers holding food or food ingredients that are removed from their original packages, such as flour, herbs, salt, sugar, and spices must be labeled with the common name of the food. New Violation. Correct By: 08/07/2024
Refrigerator door gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/27/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the kitchen and basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 07/27/2023

Protective shielding is not provided for the lights in the kitchen area. Light bulbs must be shielded, coated, or otherwise shatter-resistant in areas where there is exposed food; clean equipment, utensils, and linens; or unwrapped single-service and single-use articles. New Violation. Correct By: 07/27/2023

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with food debris and rodent droppings. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Slicer was taken out of service to clean and sanitize. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023

Various Foods were observed held at 44-50 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Foods that were out of temperature were voluntarily disposed of by the PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023

Reduced oxygen packaged salmon was observed thawing in the walk-in cooler with the packaging still intact. ROP seafood must be opened prior to thawing. Salmon was disposed. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023
Okra and produce were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023

Walk-in cooler was observed at an ambient temperature of 50 degrees Fahrenheit. Service was called and arrived during inspection. Walk-in cooler was at an ambient temperature of 40 degrees Fahrenheit at the end of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023
2 door refrigerator gaskets were observed in poor repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/27/2023

The non-food contact surfaces of the following have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris: 1. shelving in the kitchen 2. shelving in walk-in cooler 3. shelving for equipment and utensils New Violation. Correct By: 07/11/2023

Unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment or items were observed in the kitchen area and basement area. Establishments shall be kept free of unnecessary and/or non-functional equipment and litter to prevent harborage conditions. New Violation. Correct By: 07/27/2023

The following areas have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris 1. ceiling in the kitchen area 2. kitchen hoods 3. the walls near the warewashing area and bar The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023
